solution: 
```cpp
class FreqStack {
public:
    FreqStack() {
        
    }
    
    void push(int val) {
        freq[val]++;
        max_freq = max(max_freq, freq[val]);
        stacks[freq[val]].push(val);
    }
    
    int pop() {
        int val = stacks[max_freq].top();
        stacks[max_freq].pop();
        freq[val]--;
        if (stacks[max_freq].empty()) {
            max_freq--;
        }
        return val;
    }

private:
    unordered_map<int, int> freq;
    unordered_map<int, stack<int>> stacks;
    int max_freq = 0;
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(N)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses two hash maps and a stack to maintain the frequency of each element and the order of elements with the same frequency. The push operation increases the frequency of the element and adds it to the corresponding frequency stack. The pop operation removes the top element from the highest frequency stack and updates the max frequency if the stack becomes empty. This approach ensures that the most frequent element is popped, and in case of a tie, the one closest to the top is chosen.
